title: Indian-Style Pork Belly Curry
description: A rich and flavorful Indian-inspired curry made with pork belly, perfect for a special occasion. This recipe combines the tender pork with a blend of Indian spices and creamy coconut milk.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the black peppercorns, garam masala, cumin powder, sugar, and salt.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork belly, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium heat. Add a couple of tablespoons of oil and sear the pork belly until browned on all sides.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add more oil to the pot if needed, then sauté the chopped onion until it's translucent.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the coconut milk and yogurt, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the pork belly to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and caramelized.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the pork rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding any solids.
Bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat and cook until it thickens slightly.
Serve the pork belly with the sauce spooned over the top.
